title: Introduction to Computing
description: Introduces students with no prior computing experience to digital computer organization and characteristics. Covers everyday computer uses and may include electronic mail and programming in a high-level language.
prerequisites: None

title: Introduction to Computer Science I
description: First course in the computer science sequence required for all majors. Covers problem solving, computational thinking concepts including decomposition, abstraction, pattern recognition, and algorithmic thinking. Students learn pseudo-code, flowcharts, high-level programming language fundamentals including syntax, data types, variables, expressions, statements, input/output, library functions, decision making, iteration, coding, debugging, and program execution. Includes lab with programming assignments.
prerequisites: None

title: Introduction to Computer Science II
description: Second computer science course covering data structures including lists, tuples, and dictionaries; functions and modules; string handling; file input/output; exception handling; recursion; and introduction to classes and objects. Includes lab work with programming assignments using editors to compile and execute large programs.
prerequisites: COSC 111 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Computational Science and Data Analysis
description: Introduces undergraduates to computational and analytical tools/techniques for analyzing and communicating scientific knowledge through laboratory exercises and lectures reinforcing STEM concepts. Examines various software tools applicable across STEM disciplines.
prerequisites: None

title: Computer Ethics
description: Examines humanitarian, social, and professional impacts of IT, focusing on ethical issues related to networking and the Internet, IT-configured societies, moral accountability, responsibility, and liability of IT-professionals, intellectual property, privacy, security, cybercrime, theft and piracy concerns.
prerequisites: None

title: Quantum Mechanics for Computer Scientists
description: Introduces computer science majors to quantum mechanics concepts including wave functions, Heisenberg uncertainty, probabilities, superposition states, entanglement, and qubits. Covers quantum states and operators using matrices, plus the quantum nature of atoms and photons.
prerequisites: COSC 112 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Quantum Computing I
description: Introduction to quantum computation using highly parallel, quantum entangled systems. Covers mathematical and programming structures for understanding and implementing basic quantum circuits. Key topics include quantum measurement, projections, reversible computation, unitary operators, entanglement, quantum gates, gate equivalences, density matrix, and the no-cloning theorem.
prerequisites: COSC 210 and COSC 241 completed with grades of C or higher

title: Data Structures and Algorithms
description: Focuses on fundamental data structures and algorithms including strings, lists, arrays, stacks, queues, dictionaries, maps, trees, and graphs. Covers operations such as inserting, deleting, updating, traversing, searching, and sorting with time/space complexity analysis through programming implementations.
prerequisites: COSC 112 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Advanced Programming I
description: Students gain thorough knowledge of advanced programming language concepts with emphasis on structured programming. Coverage includes syntax, semantics, data types, storage classes, input/output, library functions, looping, decision making, functions, arrays, structures, unions, string handling, and file operations.
prerequisites: COSC 112 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Object Oriented Programming
description: Covers fundamental OOP concepts including abstraction, encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism. Addresses problem solving with static and dynamic data structures using an object-oriented programming language. Includes object-oriented analysis and design principles with UML.
prerequisites: COSC 112 with a grade of C or higher

title: Java Programming
description: Covers Java syntax, program structure, objects, classes, and object-oriented programming. Includes introductory data structures, annotations, self-documentation, software development principles, graphical user interfaces, and programming practice.
prerequisites: COSC 112 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Computer Systems and Digital Logic
description: Introduction to fundamental concepts of computer systems and digital logic circuit design. Covers combinational logic including Boolean algebra, DeMorgan's Theorem, logic gates, encoders, decoders, multiplexers, adders and subtractors. Addresses sequential logic such as latches, flip-flops, and counters. Includes data representation, device characteristics, and register transfer notation with emphasis on problem-solving for hardware and software design. First course in computer and assembly language programming.
prerequisites: COSC 112 and MATH 141 completed with grades of C or higher, or departmental permission

title: Computer Architecture
description: Introduction to computer organization and architecture. The computer is described as a hierarchy of levels, each performing well-defined functions. Comparisons are made in how levels and functions are implemented in various systems. Covers CPU organization and control, instruction set architecture, memory organizations, input/output structure, and parallel architectures.
prerequisites: COSC 241 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Digital Logic
description: First course in digital systems covering logic, digital circuits, and register level component design. Topics include data representation, device characteristics, and register transfer notation applied to hardware and software design, specification, design process, and graphical interfaces.
prerequisites: COSC 243 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Introduction to Data Science
description: Introduces computer use for analysis, interpretation and visualization of simple and complex, high-volume, high-dimensional and structured/unstructured data from varying sources using data analysis, modeling, data mining, data visualization and search techniques.
prerequisites: COSC 112 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Discrete Structure
description: Introduction to discrete mathematics with focus on techniques important to theoretical computer science. Topics include Boolean algebra, partially ordered structures, propositional logic, predicate logic, graphs, and coding theory.
prerequisites: COSC 112 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Quantum Computing II
description: Advanced exploration of quantum computation beyond introductory concepts. Covers applications including machine learning, linear equation solutions, search algorithms, and cryptography. Examines quantum information and computational complexity.
prerequisites: COSC 215 with a grade of C or higher

title: Algorithm Design and Analysis
description: Focuses on algorithm design and analysis methods. Topics include asymptotic complexity, divide and conquer, dynamic programming, greedy algorithms, graph algorithms, NP-hard and NP-complete problems, and approximation algorithms.
prerequisites: COSC 220 and COSC 281, both completed with grades of C or higher

title: Introduction to Cryptography
description: Modern cryptography course emphasizing fundamental aspects including digital signatures, pseudo-random number generation, and basic protocols with computational considerations. Self-contained with integrated statistical and mathematical coverage, exposing students to practical open problems in the field.
prerequisites: COSC 238 and MATH 312 completed with grades of C or higher, or instructor permission

title: Introduction to Game Design and Development
description: Familiarizes students with game development tools and practices using industry-standard resources like Unity3D. Features hands-on projects applying various game development techniques to create basic games.
prerequisites: COSC 112 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Mobile App Design & Development
description: Exploration into mobile app development through design and implementation of API clients. Students learn mobile app coding structures and design to develop mobile-based computational solutions.
prerequisites: COSC 238 completed with a grade of C or higher or department permission

title: Microprocessor Systems and Applications
description: Comprehensive study of microcomputer system components, peripheral devices, operating systems features, and application software trends. Includes hands-on experience with commercially popular microcomputers.
prerequisites: COSC 243 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Introduction to High Performance Computing
description: General introduction to high performance computing (HPC) covering parallel and distributed computing, supercomputing, architectural classification of machines and platforms (cluster, grid, cloud), program performance measurement, optimization methods, dependencies, vectorization, and parallelism.
prerequisites: COSC 112 and MATH 241 completed with grades of C or higher

title: Computer Networks
description: Covers theory and practice of computer networks, emphasizing design principles of network software and communications systems in distributed computing. Topics include network architecture, packet switching, network programming, wireless networks, data representation, channels, synchronization, and resource management.
prerequisites: COSC 243 (grade C or higher, Fall 2024) OR COSC 112 (grade C or higher, Spring 2025 onward)

title: Foundations of Computer Security and Information Assurance
description: Principles of information security relating to computer science are addressed. Topics include identification and authentication, access control, and security models. Practical aspects of security and assurance are covered for UNIX, Windows NT, and security attainment approaches.
prerequisites: COSC 241 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Cybersecurity
description: Presents threats analyzing and prediction, vulnerabilities and risks. Topics include identification and authentication, encryption and certifications, security in UNIX and Windows environments, malicious software, trusted computer systems, network attacks and defenses, firewalls, intrusion detection and prevention, database security.
prerequisites: COSC 112 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Organization of Programming Languages
description: Explores programming language concepts including syntax, grammars, data type constructs, control structures, data flow, and runtime considerations. Examples drawn from FORTRAN 90, JAVA, ALGOL, SNOBOL 4 and APL.
prerequisites: COSC 220 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Operating Systems
description: Principles underlying computer operating systems from a computer designer's perspective. Topics include process concurrence, synchronization, resource management, I/O scheduling, job and process scheduling, deadlock, semaphores, storage management, virtual memory (segmentation and paging), and contemporary operating systems. Students program in a high-level language.
prerequisites: COSC 220 and COSC 241 completed with grades of C or higher

title: Network Security Fundamentals
description: Introduces security awareness and implementation skills for networks. Covers protocols, security measures in router-based networks across platforms, attack techniques, and defensive responses.
prerequisites: COSC 349 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Foundations of Bioinformatics
description: Multidisciplinary course explaining the scope of bioinformatics, discussing computational methods, strategies and software tools for sequence analysis, and protein structure prediction.
prerequisites: COSC 112 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Introduction to Bioprogramming
description: Course involves students doing projects with computer programming in a language of their choice. No background in genetics or biology is assumed.
prerequisites: COSC 373 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Numerical Methods and Programming
description: Derives and applies techniques for approximation of numerical variables and parameters. Analyzes mathematical procedures for computational efficiency and error accumulation. Develops algorithms for numerical methods implementation in high-level computer language.
prerequisites: MATH 242 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Theory of Languages and Automata
description: Introduction to automata theory, languages, syntax, and grammar characteristics. Topics include mathematical preliminaries, the Chomsky hierarchy, finite automata, regular expressions, pushdown automata, context-free languages, and Turing machines.
prerequisites: COSC 220 and COSC 281 with grades of C or higher

title: Introduction to Quantum Computing
description: Introductory course covering quantum computing basics, quantum architecture, programming levels, quantum algorithms, and software development kits including Qiskit, Q#, Cirq, and Forest.
prerequisites: COSC 220 and MATH 312 completed with grades of C or higher

title: Quantum Cryptography
description: Focuses on quantum communications and quantum cryptography. Covers digital communications, computer networks, and classical cryptography review. Topics include encryption, key distribution protocols, and post-quantum cryptographic techniques. Students apply and test QKD protocols through projects.
prerequisites: COSC 315 and COSC 323

title: Parallel Algorithms
description: Covers fundamental concepts of concurrent programming; synchronization mechanisms based on shared variables and message passing; systematic development of correct programs; and paradigms for distributed programming.
prerequisites: COSC 220 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Capstone in Quantum Information Science
description: Students collaborate with professors on projects applying computer science to quantum phenomena or applications. Work may involve repurposing lab equipment from quantum science coursework or developing theoretical projects demonstrating quantum physics comprehension. An advisor guides project development and mentorship.
prerequisites: COSC 410 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Compilers
description: Introduction to compiler design and implementation principles, techniques, and tools. Topics include: lexical analysis (scanning), syntax analysis (parsing), semantic analysis, syntax-directed translation, intermediate code generation, code generation, code optimization, and symbol table management.
prerequisites: COSC 385 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Software Engineering
description: Covers fundamental software engineering concepts including feasibility study, development, testing, deployment, and maintenance phases following standard SDLC. Topics include formal methods, re-engineering, reverse engineering, version control, reliability, risks, software ethics, project management, and team organization. Students complete a large team-based software project.
prerequisites: COSC 220 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Database Design
description: Study of database system functions, data modeling, and logical database design. Covers query languages, query optimization, efficient data storage and access, concurrency control, and recovery.
prerequisites: COSC 220 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Computer Graphics
description: Course covering basic computer graphic devices, graphics and text modes, point plotting and line drawing, area filling, image array plotting, and mathematical generation of two and three dimensional transformations including translations, rotations, scaling, reflections, and orthogonal and perspective projections.
prerequisites: COSC 220 and MATH 241 completed with grades of C or higher

title: Introduction to Database Security
description: Focuses on security knowledge integration within organizations. Covers secure network protocol design, vulnerability discovery, ACID testing, and system vulnerability effects. Details protocols including TCP/IP, SSL/TLS, IPsec, SSH, firewalls, password policies, privileges, roles, Virtual Private Databases, and auditing. Advanced topics encompass SQL injection and DBMS security issues.
prerequisites: COSC 459 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Artificial Intelligence
description: An introduction to agent-oriented artificial intelligence covering foundations and history, intelligent agents, search techniques, knowledge and reasoning, propositional and predicate logic, forward and backward chaining, resolution, and applications including mechanical theorem proving and expert systems.
prerequisites: COSC 220 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Expert Systems
description: Study of knowledge engineering with unified treatment of computation and software design. Covers computing concepts, artificial intelligence, logic programming, and engineering in expert knowledge systems development.
prerequisites: COSC 470 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Introduction to Machine Learning
description: Data science course introducing machine learning and data mining principles. Students gain understanding of statistical learning theory, supervised and unsupervised learning, decision trees, and deep learning with practical predictive analytics solutions. Examines algorithms' role in Big Data Analysis.
prerequisites: COSC 112 and MATH 312 completed with grades of C or higher

title: Artificial Intelligence in Cybersecurity
description: Introduces undergraduate students to foundational machine learning concepts and their applications in cybersecurity. Students learn how AI enhances threat detection, automates responses, and identifies cyber risks. Topics include supervised and unsupervised learning, deep learning, explainable AI (xAI), adversarial machine learning, and ethical concerns in security AI. Hands-on labs and case studies reinforce technical and ethical understanding.
prerequisites: COSC 220 and MATH 312 completed with grades of C or higher

title: Introduction to Image Processing and Analysis
description: Covers digital image processing theory and algorithms including acquisition, formation, enhancement, color space conversion, histogram generation, segmentation, feature extraction, and clustering/classification.
prerequisites: COSC 220 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Applied Quantum Computing
description: Overview of quantum computing applications including quantum instruction sets, software development kits, and programming languages. Topics encompass cryptography, Shor's algorithm, quantum cryptography, optimization, and machine learning.
prerequisites: COSC 386 with grade C or higher

title: Senior Project
description: Graduating seniors conduct research or participate in special projects on topics reflecting new and latest developments in computer science, under faculty guidance.
prerequisites: Department Chair permission and Senior standing required to register

title: Conference Course
description: Advanced study of a computer science topic undertaken individually under department faculty guidance.
prerequisites: Permission of Department Chairperson

title: Senior Internship
description: Supervised work experience in the major at an approved off-campus site. Students engage in practical application of their field of study under departmental oversight.
prerequisites: 3.0 GPA, senior standing, and Department Chair approval required

title: Senior Research or Teaching/Tutorial Assistantship
description: Opportunity for students to gain hands-on research or teaching/tutorial experience under faculty supervision and mentorship. Limited to seniors meeting academic requirements with department approval.
prerequisites: Minimum 3.0 cumulative and major GPA; senior standing; Department Chair permission required

title: Introduction to Cloud Computing
description: Overview of cloud computing, enabling technologies, and building blocks with hands-on projects using public cloud infrastructures like Amazon Web Services. Topics include cloud infrastructures, virtualization, software-defined networks and storage, cloud storage, and programming models.
prerequisites: COSC 112 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Data Science for Social Good
description: Prepares students to work as practitioners in data science for social good. Covers statistical methods and programming for designing, developing and deploying responsible machine learning models to address social problems such as racism, addiction and domestic violence. Topics include social work data collection and processing, model training, testing and cloud deployment for use by social work professionals and the public.
prerequisites: COSC 251 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Machine Learning in the Cloud
description: Introduces concepts and terminology of machine learning in cloud environments, focusing on forecasting, computer vision, and natural language processing. Students learn to select and apply machine learning services for business problems, label, build, train, and deploy custom models through hands-on approaches. Topics include data collection, evaluation, feature engineering, model training, evaluation, and tuning.
prerequisites: COSC 251 completed with a grade of C or higher

title: Cloud Applications
description: Covers cloud application fundamentals, including terminology and models. Explores key tasks like email, file storage, word processing, and data collection. Examines major benefits such as scalability, reduced costs, and improved data sharing. Discusses Cloud APIs and popular platforms including Google Apps, Facebook, and YouTube.
prerequisites: CLCO 261 with grade of C or higher

title: Data Analytics in Cloud
description: Overview of Big Data applications, OpenStack, Apache Hadoop Ecosystem, MapReduce programming model, and Spark framework. Covers applications in bioinformatics, machine learning, and business domains including marketing analytics, retail, hospitality, consumer goods, and defense. Focuses on cloud computing for manipulating, storing, and analyzing big data.
prerequisites: CLCO 261 with grade of C or higher

title: Senior Project in Cloud Computing
description: Explores cutting-edge technologies in software and hardware through advanced cloud computing projects. Topics include SaaS, IaaS, PaaS, MapReduce, Hadoop clusters, BitTorrent protocols, security issues in cloud transactions, mobile cloud applications, and multi-cloud disaster recovery. Students select projects in consultation with faculty advisors.
prerequisites: Department Chair permission and Senior Standing required
